content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to find factorial of a number using recursion.


Input Format
The only line of input contains a single integer NNN.



Output Format
Print the factorial of the given number.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the factorial of N on a separate line.

solution.approach: The factorial of a non‑negative integer N is defined as N! = N·(N‑1)·...·1 with 0! = 1. Because N ≤ 20, the result fits into a 64‑bit signed integer. For each test case we compute N! using a simple recursive function factorial(n) that returns 1 when n ≤ 1 and otherwise returns n * factorial(n‑1). The recursion depth never exceeds 20, so it is safe. We read all test cases, compute the answer independently and output them. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case (N ≤ 20) and uses O(N) call‑stack space, which is negligible for the given limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (N ≤ 20), effectively O(T) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) recursion depth, at most 20 call frames, i.e., O(1) extra memory.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static long factorial(int n){if(n<=1)return 1L;return n*factorial(n-1);}public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int i=0;i<T;i++){String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()){}int N=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());sb.append(factorial(N));if(i+1<T)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
